shortdesc.p: The Xilinx Software Development Kit (SDK) provides a complete environment for creating software applications targeted for Xilinx embedded processors. It includes a GNU-based compiler toolchain, JTAG debugger, flash programmer, middleware libraries, bare-metal BSPs and drivers for Xilinx IP. Xilinx SDK also includes a robust IDE for C/C++ bare-metal and Linux application development and debugging. Based upon the open source Eclipse platform, SDK incorporates the C/C++ Development Toolkit (CDT).

topic.title: Key Concepts for Using the Xilinx SDK

shortdesc.p: Xilinx and its Alliance Members deliver embedded tools and runtime environments designed to enable you to efficiently and quickly move from concept to release. We provide you with all the components needed to create your embedded system using Xilinx Zynq SoC and MPSoC devices, and MicroBlaze processor cores, including tools for profiling and debug, open source and bare metal drivers, and multiple runtime & Multi-OS environments.

The Hardware Platform Specification file captures all the information and files from a Xilinx® Embedded Development Kit (EDK) hardware design that is required for a software developer to write, debug, and deploy software applications for that hardware. SDK uses the Hardware Description file to detect the processor(s) and memory mapped peripherals present in the hardware.

topic.title: Creating a New Hardware Platform Specification
Importing a Hardware Platform Specification File
Exporting a Hardware Specification in Vivado

shortdesc.p: Projects are your final application containers. The project directory that is created contains (or links to) your C/C++ source files, executable output file, and associated utility files, such as the make files used to build the project.

topic.title: Using Custom Libraries in Application Projects
Creating a Zynq Boot Image for an Application
Opening the Create Boot Image Dialog Box
Working with Boot Image Format Files
Creating a New Zynq FSBL Application Project
Specifying Debug and Optimization Compiler Flags
Generating a Linker Script for an Application
Requirements for Importing a Project into a Workspace
Importing Resources from the File System
Importing Resources from an Archive File
Exporting Resources to the File System
Exporting Resources to an Archive File
Using SDK with Revision Control Software

shortdesc.p: A board support package (BSP) is a collection of libraries and drivers that will form the lowest layer of your application software stack. Your software applications must link against or run on top of a given software platform using the APIs that it provides. Therefore, before you can create and use software applications in SDK, you must create a board support package.

topic.title: Using the Board Support Package Overview Page
Using the Board Support Package Settings Page
Using the Board Support Package Drivers Page
Using the Board Support Package Settings Driver Configuration Page
Building a Board Support Package for SDK
Creating a Board Support Package (External Tools)
Configuring a Board Support Package (External Tools)
Generating a Board Support Package for External Tools
Re-Targeting A BSP for a New Hardware Platform

shortdesc.p: The Xilinx System Debugger (XSDB) in the SDK is an integrated debugger supporting Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC, Zynq-7000 AP SoC and MicroBlaze core. It supports all the common debug features such as setting breakpoints or watchpoints, stepping through program execution, viewing the program variables and stack, and viewing the contents of the memory in the system. It can also simultaneously debug programs running on different processors (in a multi-processor system), all from within the same debug environment.

topic.title: Creating or Editing a Launch Configuration
Standalone Application Debug Using Xilinx System Debugger
Linux Application Debugging with System Debugger
My application already exists in the Linux target. How can I tell system debugger to use my existing application, instead of downloading the application?
I want to debug all the applications that are running on my Linux target. How can I achieve it?
Attach and Debug using Xilinx System Debugger
Standalone Application Debug using System Debugger on QEMU
Using a Remote Host with System Debugger

shortdesc.p: Xilinx Software Commandline Tool (XSCT) is an interactive and scriptable commandline interface to Xilinx Software Development Kit.

topic.title: Installing and Launching XSCT on Windows
Installing and Launching XSCT on Linux
Changing Compiler Options of an Application Project
Creating an Application Project Using an Application Template
Creating a Bootable Image and Program the Flash
Debugging a Program Already Running on the Target
Debugging Applications on Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC
Running an Application in Non-Interactive Mode
Switching Between XSCT and Xilinx SDK Development Environment

shortdesc.p: Profiling tools in SDK help identify bottle necks in your code occurring due to the interaction of functions that are executed within the programmable logic, and on the processor. SDK supports hierarchical profiling - allowing the user to view which called functions, or which calling functions are affecting processor performance the most.

topic.title: Setting Up the Hardware for Profiling
Setting Up the Software for Profiling
Setting up the Board Support Package
Profiling Linux Applications with System Debugger

shortdesc.p: Creating high-performing SoC based systems takes time and requires insight into the interactions between the software and the peripherals. Tools that allow you to gather performance data and visualize your system are critical to achieving performance targets. The Xilinx System Performance Analysis toolbox (available in the XSDK) allows you to model, measure, analyze and optimize your system.

topic.title: System Performance Modeling Using the SDK Predefined Design
Creating the System Performance Modeling Project
System Performance Modeling Using a User-Defined Flow
What’s New in Xilinx SDK v2018.1
What’s New in Xilinx SDK (Earlier Releases)

Projects created in the default location may cause compilation issues

Ucos application project creations fail due to non-availability of pre-compiled libraries and source code
Debugging or running of A53 32-bit application requires board reset
MSS Viewer does not refresh automatically
Renaming hardware platforms causes compilation errors
Restore defaults in C/C++ Build Settings causes settings to be lost
SDK does not display correct menus, icons or perspective
Debug System Reset doesn’t work on a 2.0. Zc706 board that has the 2.0 silicon
SDK Hangs After Design Changes in Block Design and Export Hardware (Design with User IP)
While Initializing the bitstream with an ELF file, SDK uses bootloop

How can I set the register display format so that it defaults to "Hexadecimal"?
Can I debug more than one application at the same time?
I have a large ELF application file to debug, and SDK seems to time out when I am downloading the ELF file. How can I increase the time-out period?

I have my board connected on one machine, and I would like to debug my application using SDK running on another machine. How do I set up the Launch Configuration to perform this remote debug operation?
I want to debug an ELF file using SDK without creating a C/C++ Project. How do I do that?
During Debugging (or Running) the application, I would like to download my data files along with my application's ELF file. How do I do this?
How do I specify the Reset options to be used when a Debug (or Run) session is launched?
How can I tell Xilinx System debugger to start at program entry?
Debug after re-launch does not stop on breakpoints.
Cannot set more than 5 breakpoints/watchpoints for ARM targets
Launching hw_server gives a "missing FTD2XX.dll" error message.
